comprehensive_summary:
Jean Racine is a 17th-century French neoclassical tragedian best known for his play Phedre, which is by far his most frequently clued work. He is consistently identified alongside Moliere and Pierre Corneille as one of the three great French dramatists of the period, and is particularly associated with his use of alexandrine verse (lines of twelve syllables divided by a caesura into two hemistichs of six).

Phedre, his most famous tragedy, depicts the title character's incestuous love for her stepson Hippolytus (Hippolyte), the son of her husband Theseus (Thesee). Key plot points include Phedre confessing her love to her nurse Oenone, who then concocts a scheme to accuse Hippolytus of raping Phedre. Theseus invokes Neptune's curse against his own son, leading to a climactic scene in which Hippolytus's chariot is attacked by a horned sea monster (described by Theramene as half-bull, half-snake), causing the horses to drag him to his death. Oenone drowns herself, and Phedre poisons herself. The play introduces the character Aricie as Hippolytus's love interest, a notable difference from its source, Euripides' Hippolytus. Theophile Gautier called the line describing Phedre as 'the daughter of Minos and Pasiphae' Racine's finest (or only 'tolerable') verse, and it is frequently described as untranslatable. Ted Hughes spent his last months translating the play.

Andromaque is his second most clued tragedy, adapted from the Trojan War story. In it, Hermione asks her cousin Orestes to kill Pyrrhus, who loves the title character Andromaque. After Pyrrhus dies, Hermione vows to kill herself, which causes Orestes to go insane. The play includes nobly-born servant characters Cleone and Cephisa. The role of Andromaque was written for actress Therese du Parc, Racine's lover, whom he controversially stole from Moliere's company.

Athalie is another major tragedy, which Voltaire called 'the supreme achievement of the human mind.' In this play, the high priest Joad and Jehoshebath conspire to dethrone the title queen, who worships Baal, and install Joash (Joas) as king of the Jews. The play includes extensive musical choruses. Racine was commissioned to write it and Esther as religious pieces for Madame de Maintenon's girls' boarding school.

Britannicus is a tragedy about the Roman Emperor Nero, who abducts Junia (fiancee of the title character) and poisons Britannicus at a feast. At the end, Junia becomes a Vestal Virgin, and Nero's tutor Narcisse is killed by an angry mob.

Esther is a three-act play (unconventional for Racine) in which the title character convinces King Ahasuerus to stop a plotted massacre of Jews, with Hydaspes and Asaph as officers of the king.

Les Plaideurs (The Litigants) is Racine's only comedy, adapted from Aristophanes' The Wasps. It features the mad Judge Dandin, the porter Petit Jean, and culminates when a judge rules against a dog named Citron for eating a chicken but pardons it after pleas from its puppies. Leander and Isabelle trick Chicaneau and Dandin into allowing their marriage.

Racine was also the subject of a major literary controversy when Roland Barthes published a psychoanalysis-inflected study (On Racine), which was attacked by Raymond Picard in the article 'New Criticism or New Fraud?' Racine's response through Criticism and Truth helped legitimize literary theory as a discipline. Lucien Goldmann's study The Hidden God compared the decline of Racine's social class to how his characters reject the world in which they are compelled to live. In Proust's In Search of Lost Time, the narrator befriends Berma, an actress specializing in Racine's plays, and schoolgirls are assigned to write a letter from Sophocles to Racine from the underworld. Racine sided with the Ancients in the Quarrel between the Ancients and the Moderns. He converted to Jansenism late in life and spent his final years writing the Spiritual Canticles. He closely adhered to the dramatic unities.

works.name: Phedre
works.description: Phedre is Racine's most famous tragedy, depicting the title character's incestuous love for her stepson Hippolytus. The play is based on Euripides' Hippolytus but introduces the character Aricie as Hippolytus's love interest. Key plot points include Phedre's confession to nurse Oenone, Theseus invoking Neptune's curse, the sea monster attacking Hippolytus's chariot (described by Theramene), Oenone drowning, and Phedre poisoning herself. The famous line describing Phedre as 'the daughter of Minos and Pasiphae' is repeatedly clued as untranslatable.

clues.clue: Phedre described as 'the daughter of Minos and Pasiphae' -- called Racine's finest/only tolerable line by Theophile Gautier; described as untranslatable
quotes: Theophile Gautier claimed that this writer's only 'tolerable' verse was the opening line 'the daughter of Minos and Pasiphae'.
This playwright wrote a classic alexandrine describing a title character as 'daughter of Minos and Pasiphae.'

clues.clue: Hippolytus's chariot attacked by a sea monster (half-bull, half-snake/horned), described offstage by Theramene; horses drag Hippolytus to death
quotes: A chariot is attacked by a horned sea monster at the end of a play by this author.
In Act 5 of one of his plays, Theramene describes the protagonist's horses running into the sea after being attacked by a sea monster.
the appearance of a half-bull, half-snake monster causes Hippolyte to be dragged to his death

clues.clue: The nurse Oenone convinces Phedre to declare her love for Hippolytus / hatches the plot to accuse Hippolytus of rape; Oenone later drowns herself
quotes: the maid Oenone convinces the title character to declare her love for her stepson Hippolytus
the wife of Theseus accuses her stepson Hippolytus of rape after he ignores her advances

clues.clue: Title character's incestuous love for stepson Hippolytus, wife of Theseus
quotes: uses characteristic rhymed alexandrines to depict the title character's incestuous love for Hippolyte
the wife of Theseus accuses Hippolytus of raping her after he rejects her incestuous advances

clues.clue: Phedre poisons herself after Hippolytus's death / after Theseus discovers her love
quotes: The title character of that play kills herself after her husband Thesee discovers her love for Hippolyte.
the title character poisons herself after her nurse Oenone hatches a plot

clues.clue: Aricie is Hippolytus's love interest, a notable addition not in Euripides' Hippolytus
quotes: This play introduces the character Aricie, whose love Hippolytus is incestuously loved by the title character.
A notable difference between Euripides' Hippolytus and Racine's Phedre is the latter's inclusion of this character, Hippolytus' love interest.

clues.clue: Based on Euripides' Hippolytus
quotes: Racine's Phedre was based on this ancient Greek writer's play Hippolytus.
another plot also derived from Euripides

clues.clue: Theseus invokes Neptune's curse against his own son
quotes: this author wrote a play in which a king invokes Neptune's curse against his own son

clues.clue: Ted Hughes spent his last months translating Phedre
quotes: Ted Hughes spent his last months translating the rhymed alexandrine verse of this author's play

clues.clue: Theramene described as Hippolytus's lecherous tutor
quotes: the target of that love is tutored by the lecherous Theramene

clues.clue: 'Like virtue, crime has its degrees' -- line from the play
quotes: One of his characters declares 'like virtue, crime has its degrees.'

works.name: Andromaque
works.description: Andromaque is Racine's retelling of the Trojan War aftermath. Hermione asks Orestes to kill Pyrrhus, who loves the title character Andromaque. After Pyrrhus dies, Hermione kills herself, and Orestes goes insane. The role was written for actress Therese du Parc, Racine's lover. The play features nobly-born servants Cleone and Cephisa, and Andromaque marries Pyrrhus to protect her son Astyanax.

clues.clue: Hermione asks Orestes to kill Pyrrhus, who loves the title character; after Pyrrhus dies, Hermione kills herself and Orestes goes insane
quotes: In one of this playwright's works, Hermione asks her cousin to kill Pyrrhus, who loves the title character.
Hermione vows to kill herself after Pyrrhus dies, which results in Orestes going insane.

clues.clue: Hermione tells Cleone she 'loved Pyrrhus too much not to hate him'
quotes: Hermione tells Cleone that she 'loved' Pyrrhus 'too much not to hate him,' before asking Orestes to kill him.

clues.clue: Title role written for actress Therese du Parc, Racine's lover, whom he stole from Moliere's company
quotes: This author of the tragedies Athalie and Berenice wrote the title role of his play, Andromaque, for actress Therese du Parc.
Racine wrote the title role in Andromaque for this woman, while they were lovers

clues.clue: Andromaque marries Pyrrhus to protect her son Astyanax
quotes: the title character's marriage to Pyrrhus in order to protect her son Astyanax

clues.clue: Features nobly-born servants Cleone and Cephisa (Cephise)
quotes: This author included the nobly-born servants Cleone and Cephisa in another play.
this author returned to writing 'suivante' roles such as Cephise and Cleone

works.name: Athalie
works.description: Athalie is a biblical tragedy that Voltaire called 'the supreme achievement of the human mind.' The high priest Joad and Jehoshebath conspire to dethrone the title queen, who worships Baal, and install Joash as king of the Jews. The title character describes her mother wearing makeup to 'repair the irreparable ravages of time.' Racine was commissioned to write it for Madame de Maintenon's girls' school.

clues.clue: Voltaire called it 'the supreme achievement of the human mind'
quotes: Voltaire called his Athalie the greatest triumph of the human mind.
Voltaire called the 'supreme achievement of the human mind'

clues.clue: High priest Joad installs Joash (Joas) as king; title queen worships Baal and is dethroned/killed
quotes: Joad and Jehoshebath agree to dethrone the title character for her worship of Baal.
the high priest Joad attempts to install Joash on the throne to replace the title character, who has abandoned Judaism to worship Baal

clues.clue: Written for Madame de Maintenon's girls' boarding school (along with Esther)
quotes: This author was commissioned to write religious pieces for a girl's boarding school by Madame de Maintenon, resulting in his work Esther.
This playwright came out of retirement to write two Old Testament plays for the Madame de Maintenon, Esther and Athalie.

clues.clue: Includes extensive musical choruses
quotes: That play by this man, Esther, includes extensive musical choruses, which he also used in a biblical drama

clues.clue: Title character describes her mother wearing makeup to 'repair the irreparable ravages of time'
quotes: the title character of this play describes her mother wearing makeup to 'repair the irreparable ravages of time'

works.name: Britannicus
works.description: Britannicus depicts the Roman Emperor Nero, who abducts Junia (the title character's fiancee) and poisons Britannicus at a feast. At the end, Junia becomes a Vestal Virgin and Nero's tutor Narcisse is killed by an angry mob. A woman predicts her son will murder her next.

clues.clue: Nero poisons the title character and abducts his fiancee Junia; Junia becomes a Vestal Virgin
quotes: Junia becomes a priestess and the emperor poisons the title character at a feast.
Nero abducts a young woman who is betrothed to the title character.

clues.clue: An angry mob kills Narcisse (Nero's tutor) at the end
quotes: another character's tutor is killed by a mob
concludes with Junia promising to become a vestal virgin and an angry mob killing Narcisse

clues.clue: A woman predicts her son will murder her next and try to drown his remorse by committing fresh atrocities
quotes: a woman predicts that her own son will murder her next, and then try to drown his remorse for that murder by committing fresh atrocities

works.name: Les Plaideurs (The Litigants)
works.description: Les Plaideurs is Racine's only comedy, adapted from Aristophanes' The Wasps. It features the mad Judge Dandin, the porter Petit Jean, and includes a trial of the dog Citron for eating a chicken. Leander and Isabelle trick Chicaneau and Dandin into allowing their marriage.

clues.clue: Racine's only comedy; adapted from Aristophanes' The Wasps
quotes: The only comedic play by this author
Racine's only comedy, The Litigants, adapts this Greek author's play The Wasps.

clues.clue: Judge rules against a dog (Citron) for eating a chicken, but pardons it after pleas from its puppies
quotes: culminates when a judge rules against a dog for eating a chicken, but pardons it after pleas from its puppies
Le Souffler, a prompter, in acting as prosecutor in the trial of the dog Citron

clues.clue: Features the mad Judge Dandin and porter Petit Jean
quotes: That work centers on Dandin, a mad judge.
the porter Petit Jean is forced to stand guard rather than sleep

works.name: Other Works
works.description: Racine's other clued works include Esther (a three-act play about the biblical Esther convincing King Ahasuerus to stop a massacre of Jews), Berenice (about the Roman Emperor Titus renouncing the Queen of Judaea because 'Rome took a hatred to the name of King'), Bajazet, and Mithridates (about Xiphares and Monime inheriting the kingdom). La Thebaide was his first play performed live, premiered by Moliere's troupe in 1664.

clues.clue: Esther: three-act play; title character convinces King Ahasuerus to stop a plotted massacre of Jews; written for Madame de Maintenon's school
quotes: King Ahasuerus, who is convinced by the title character to stop a plotted massacre of Jews
wrote a play about Esther

clues.clue: Berenice: Roman Emperor Titus forced to renounce the Queen of Judaea because 'Rome took a hatred to the name of King'
quotes: the Roman Emperor Titus is forced to renounce the Queen of Judaea
a woman's rejection of Antiochus and Titus

clues.clue: La Thebaide: first play performed live, premiered by Moliere's troupe in 1664
quotes: In 1664, Moliere's troupe premiered La Thebaide, the first of this author's plays to be performed live.

clues.clue: Mithridates: Xiphares and Monime inherit the kingdom
quotes: wrote about Xiphares and Monime inheriting the kingdom of Mithridates in another work

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Racine is identified as a 17th-century French neoclassical tragedian, known for his alexandrine verse (twelve-syllable lines with a caesura) and strict adherence to the dramatic unities. He was a contemporary of Moliere and Corneille. Roland Barthes wrote a psychoanalysis-inflected study On Racine, which was attacked by Raymond Picard in 'New Criticism or New Fraud?' -- the resulting controversy helped legitimize literary theory. Lucien Goldmann's The Hidden God analyzed Racine's social class. In Proust's In Search of Lost Time, the actress Berma specializes in Racine's plays. Racine sided with the Ancients in the Quarrel between Ancients and Moderns. He converted to Jansenism late in life.

clues.clue: French neoclassical tragedian; author of Andromaque, Phedre, etc.
quotes: name this French neoclassical playwright of Phedre
name this seventeenth-century French tragedian of Berenice, Andromaque, and Phedre

clues.clue: Used alexandrine verse (twelve syllables, caesura dividing two hemistichs of six)
quotes: his characteristic rhyming alexandrines
This master of alexandrine verse

clues.clue: Roland Barthes' On Racine attacked by Raymond Picard in 'New Criticism or New Fraud?'; the controversy helped legitimize literary theory
quotes: The essay 'New Criticism or New Fraud?' attacked a nontraditional book about this author, prompting a response in Criticism and Truth.
The furor surrounding Raymond Picard's polemic against a book 'on' this author by Roland Barthes is credited with legitimizing theory as a discipline.

clues.clue: Lucien Goldmann's The Hidden God compares the decline of Racine's social class to his characters rejecting the world
quotes: The decline of this author's social class is compared to the way that his characters reject the world in which they are compelled to live in Lucien Goldmann's study of this author, The Hidden God.

clues.clue: In Proust's In Search of Lost Time, the narrator befriends Berma, an actress specializing in Racine's plays; schoolgirls assigned to write letters from Sophocles to Racine
quotes: The narrator of Proust's In Search of Lost Time befriends Berma, an actress specializing in this man's plays.
a group of schoolgirls are assigned to write a letter from Sophocles to this writer from the underworld

clues.clue: Converted to Jansenism; spent last years writing Spiritual Canticles
quotes: This author converted to Jansenism and spent the last years of his life writing the Spiritual Canticles.

clues.clue: Sided with the Ancients in the Quarrel between Ancients and Moderns
quotes: This playwright generally sided with the Ancients in the quarrel.

clues.clue: Known for small vocabulary and simple diction; characters 'trapped' by passion
quotes: This author was known for his small vocabulary and simple diction, depicting characters 'trapped' by passion.

clues.clue: Closely adhered to the dramatic unities (time, place, action)
quotes: Racine's plays very closely adhere to this set of three classical rules of drama

clues.clue: Alan Hollinghurst's best-known translations are of Racine's plays; John Cairncross (Cambridge Five spy) translated for Penguin Classics
quotes: Alan Hollinghurst's best-known translations are of this non-Greek author's plays.
Plays by this author and an alphabetically-prior contemporary were popularised in Penguin Classics translations by Cambridge Five spy John Cairncross.
